title: Celestia Galactica Photografica
content:
{{Top|fa|rwm|lgc}}
{{ComicStory
|image=[[File:Celestia_title.png]]
|title="Celestia Galactica Photografica"
|writer=[[Ryan Church]]<ref name="Legends">''[[Star Wars Legends Epic Collection: Legacy Vol. 4]]''</ref>
|penciller=
|inker=
|letterer=Ryan Church<ref name="Legends" />
|colorist=Ryan Church<ref name="Legends" />
|illustrator=Ryan Church<ref name="CGP">{{StoryCite|book=Star Wars: Visionaries|story=Celestia Galactica Photografica}}</ref>
|cover artist=
|editor=[[Jeremy Barlow]]<ref name="CGP" />
|publisher=[[Dark Horse Comics]]<ref name="CGP" />
|release date=[[April 2]], [[2005]]<ref name="Preview">{{SWArchive|url=eu/lit/comics/news20050315.html|text=Inside ''Star Wars: Visionaries''}}</ref>
|published in=''[[Star Wars: Visionaries]]''<ref name="CGP" />
|reprinted in=*''[[Star Wars Omnibus: Wild Space Volume 2]]''<ref name="Omnibus">''[[Star Wars Omnibus: Wild Space Volume 2]]''</ref>
*''[[Star Wars Legends Epic Collection: Legacy Vol. 4]]''<ref name="Legends" />
|pages=12<ref name="CGP" />
|timeline=During or after [[104 ABY]]<ref name="104 ABY">"[[Celestia Galactica Photografica]]" was included in ''[[Star Wars Legends Epic Collection: Legacy Vol. 4]]'', which describes the stories it contains as taking place more than a [[Standard century/Legends|century]] after the events of [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Star Wars'': Episode VI ''Return of the Jedi'']]. That film took place in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]] according to ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'', meaning that the [[Painting/Legends|paintings]] in "Celestia Galactica Photografica" must have been created after [[104 ABY]].</ref>
|series=
|preceded by=*"[[Deep Forest]]" {{C|''Star Wars: Visionaries''}}<ref name="Visionaries">''[[Star Wars: Visionaries]]''</ref>
*"[[Storyteller (comic story)|Storyteller]]" {{C|Legends Epic Collection}}<ref name="Legends" />
|followed by=*"[[The Eyes of Revolution]]" {{C|''Star Wars: Visionaries''}}<ref name="Visionaries" />
*"[[The Fourth Precept]]" {{C|Legends Epic Collection}}<ref name="Legends" />
}}
"'''Celestia Galactica Photografica'''," also known as "'''Celestia Galactica'''," is a twelve-page gallery by [[Ryan Church]] featured in the graphic novel anthology ''[[Star Wars: Visionaries]]'', which was published by [[Dark Horse Comics]] in [[2005]]. The gallery consists of a series of twelve [[in-universe]] [[Painting/Legends|paintings]] from various locations and events in the ''[[Star Wars]]'' [[The galaxy/Legends|galaxy]], created over [[Standard century/Legends|one hundred]] [[Standard year/Legends|years]] after the events of the [[original trilogy]] film [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Star Wars'': Episode VI ''Return of the Jedi'']]. Eras featured include the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] and [[Imperial Era/Legends|Imperial Period]], while other paintings take place seemingly long after the events of the original trilogy films.

The comics in ''Visionaries'' came about from a desire by author [[Jonathan W. Rinzler|J.W. Rinzler]] to highlight the unused works created by concept artists working on the [[prequel trilogy]] film [[Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith|''Star Wars'': Episode III ''Revenge of the Sith'']]. Two images from "Celestia Galactica Photografica," ''[[Mygeeto Burns]]'' and ''[[Utapau Surrenders]]'', were used as the front and back covers of ''Visionaries'' respectively.

==Publisher's summary==
''"''A series of full page paintings capture the amazing vistas around the galaxy, including a battle on Mygeeto, refugees on Alderaan, an immense Jedi museum and much more.''"<ref name="Preview" />''

==Contents==
[[File:Alderaan_Refugees.png|thumb|left|200px|''Alderaan Refugees'']]
"Celestia Galactica Photografica" is a collection of twelve [[Painting/Legends|paintings]] showcasing various events and locations throughout [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. The first painting is ''[[Mygeeto Burns]]'', portraying the [[Battle of Mygeeto]] during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]. The image shows a [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]]-wielding [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] standing before a [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Separatist]] [[LM-432 crab droid/Legends|LM-432 crab droid]] inside a [[Fire/Legends|burning]] [[Mygeeto/Legends|Mygeetan]] [[City/Legends|city]]. In the background, more Jedi engage in [[Lightsaber combat/Legends|combat]] with more crab [[Droid/Legends|droids]]. ''[[Alderaan Refugees]]'' shows a host of vehicles parked in a [[Color/Legends|green]] area of the planet [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaan]]. On one [[Starship/Legends|starship]], a group of [[Human/Legends|Humans]] gather around a fire, while others can be seen setting up tents. In ''[[Death Star Drydock]]'', [[Imperial-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial''-class Star Destroyers]] are receiving repairs inside a [[Death Star/Legends|Death Star]]. An [[Imperial officer/Legends|Imperial officer]] and the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] appear to be overseeing the repairs on a platform strewn with loose [[Paper/Legends|paper]]. In ''[[AT-AT Villa]]'', two [[All Terrain Armored Transport/Legends|All Terrain Armored Transports]] have been modified to host various buildings on a world with rocky formations. Below them, one [[Alien/Legends|alien]] guides a starship onto a landing platform while another converses with two droids below.<ref name="CGP" />

''[[Green Coruscant]]'' depicts the [[Ecumenopolis/Legends|cityscape]] of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] partially replaced with [[Grass/Legends|grass]] and [[Tree/Legends|trees]]. Several floating structures are present in the painting and colorful [[Speeder/Legends|speeders]] are parked throughout, with their inhabitants appearing to observe the natural environment. ''[[Reclaimed Wreck]]'' portrays an ''Imperial''-class [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] crashed on a [[Forest/Legends|forested]] world. ''[[Bad Pet]]'' portrays a group of four [[Rancor/Legends|rancors]] rampaging through an Imperial [[Hangar/Legends|hangar]], with one holding a crushed [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE/LN starfighter]]. A group of [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] stands before the beasts while Vader points towards them. ''[[Bridgeworld Lost]]'' also takes place during the Clone Wars, featuring [[Skakoan/Legends|Skakoans]] on a world containing [[Bridge city/Legends|bridge cities]] surrendering to [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone troopers]] of the [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic]]. ''[[Executor Executes]]'' portrays the [[Super Star Destroyer/Legends|Super Star Destroyer]] [[Executor/Legends|''Executor'']] and other Star Destroyers attacking an artificial ring surrounding an astronomical object.<ref name="CGP" />

[[File:Jedi_Museum.png|thumb|right|200px|''Jedi Museum'']]
''[[Jedi Museum]]'' depicts the interior of a large museum containing artifacts associated with the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]], observed by dozens of patrons throughout the building's many levels. Visible is a statue of a Jedi raising a lightsaber while riding a [[Varactyl/Legends|varactyl]] and a [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|T-65 X-wing starfighter]]. More statues of Jedi appear on the highest level of the museum. ''[[Hunting Overlook]]'' portrays a giant tree inhabited by the [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiees]] on the planet [[Kashyyyk/Legends|Kashyyyk]]. In the foreground, a group of Wookiee mechanics work on a [[Raddaugh Gnasp fluttercraft/Legends|Raddaugh Gnasp fluttercraft]], and a group of colored [[YT-1300 light freighter/Legends|YT-1300 light freighters]] take flight around the tree. Lastly, ''[[Utapau Surrenders]]'' portrays the capitulation of the planet Utapau to the [[Separatist Droid Army/Legends|Separatist Droid Army]] under the command of the [[Kaleesh/Legends|Kaleesh]] [[General/Legends|General]] [[Grievous/Legends|Grievous]] during the Clone Wars. Taking place inside one of the planet's [[Sinkhole/Legends|sinkholes]], a [[Clothing/Legends|robed]] [[Pau'an/Legends|Pau'an]] kneels before Grievous, presenting him with a [[Staff/Legends|staff]] while hundreds of other Pau'ans watch on. In the background, three [[Core ship/Legends|core ships]] descend into the sinkhole, with one unloading dozens of [[Vulture-class starfighter/Legends|vulture droids]].<ref name="CGP" />

==Development==
{{Quote|Nevertheless, it seemed a shame not to give a voice to those artists who had perhaps more to say — to those who had become initiates in an arcane if popular artform. An obvious solution was to create a medium for this group to tell their own ''Star Wars'' stories. The result is ''Star Wars: Visionaries'' — intimate views by some of the key creators of a galaxy far, far away…|J.W. Rinzler, on the origins of ''Star Wars: Visionaries''|<ref name="Visionaries" />}}
[[File:Swvis1cover.jpg|thumb|left|250px|The cover of ''Star Wars: Visionaries'', which uses ''Mygeeto Burns'' from "Celestia Galactica Photografica."]]
"Celestia Galactica Photografica" was created,<ref name="CGP" /> written, illustrated, colored, and lettered<ref name="Legends" /> by concept artist [[Ryan Church]] for ''[[Star Wars: Visionaries]]'', a graphic novel anthology completed by concept artists for the [[prequel trilogy]] film [[Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith|''Star Wars'': Episode III ''Revenge of the Sith'']],<ref name="Visionaries" /> which was released on [[May 19]], [[2005]].<ref name="Year by Year">''[[Star Wars Year By Year: A Visual History, New Edition]]''</ref> The concept for ''Visionaries'' was pitched by author [[Jonathan W. Rinzler|J.W. Rinzler]], who wanted to honor the work completed by the film's artists that would have otherwise gone unseen.<ref name="Insider">{{InsiderCite|82|Drawn by the Force}}</ref> Those involved had free reign to choose whichever aspect of the ''[[Star Wars]]'' universe they wished to explore.<ref name="Comics Companion">''[[Star Wars: The Comics Companion]]''</ref> The announcement of ''Visionaries'' on [[StarWars.com]] on [[August 3]], [[2004]] also revealed Church's involvement.<ref name="Visionaries announcement">{{SWArchive|url=eu/lit/comics/news20040803.html|text=Coming Soon: ''Star Wars: Visionaries''}}</ref> Another StarWars.com post on [[March 15]], 2005, revealed "Celestia Galactica Photografica" as part of the collection in ''Visionaries'' and provided a brief overview of some of the images, alluding to ''Mygeeto Burns'', ''Alderaan Refugees'', and ''Jedi Museum''.<ref name="Preview" />

Some of the concepts developed for ''Revenge of the Sith'' made appearances in "Celestia Galactica Photografica." In that film's pre-production phase, the "bridge world" was planned to be the planet [[Saleucami/Legends|Saleucami]], associated with the [[Techno Union/Legends|Techno Union]] and therefore the Separatists. Ultimately, by the time of the film's release it was modified to become the planet [[Cato Neimoidia/Legends|Cato Neimoidia]] and associated with a different Confederate organization: the [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]].<ref name="Cato Neimoidia">{{DB|location|catoneimoidia|Cato Neimoidia}}</ref> Another concept art piece by Church in [[2002]] was titled "[[:File:Mygeeto Surrender.jpg|Mygeeto Surrender]]" and featured [[Muun/Legends|Muun]] members of the [[InterGalactic Banking Clan/Legends|InterGalactic Banking Clan]] surrendering to clone troopers,<ref name="Insider 86">{{InsiderCite|84|The Art of Revenge of the Sith}}</ref> similar to "Bridgeworld Lost."<ref name="CGP" /> Additionally in 2002, Church had also developed the concept of an artificial ring surrounding a planet,<ref name="The Unkown">{{SW|url=series/clone-wars/the-unknown-trivia-gallery|text="The Unknown" Trivia Gallery}} {{C|Slide 1}}</ref> which is visible in ''AT-AT Villa'' and ''Executor Executes''.<ref name="CGP" />

==Release and reception==
[[File:Ryan_Church.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Ryan Church illustrated "Celestia Galactica Photografica."]]
''Visionaries'' was edited by [[Jeremy Barlow]] and published as a trade paperback by [[Dark Horse Comics]] on [[April 2]], 2005.<ref name="Preview" /> ''Mygeeto Burns'' additionally served as the front cover art for ''Visionaries'', while ''Utapau Surrenders'' appeared on the back cover. In the anthology, "Celestia Galactica Photografica" is placed tenth, between the stories "[[Deep Forest]]" and "[[The Eyes of Revolution]]." The contents page of the graphic novel uses the shortened name "Celestia Galactica."<ref name="Visionaries" /> Barlow praised the quality and variety of the works in ''Visionaries'', believing that the creators involved had justified their positions as artists working for<ref name="Insider" /> ''Star Wars'' creator<ref name="Year by Year" />  [[George Lucas]].<ref name="Insider" />

"Celestia Galactica Photografica," along with the rest of ''Visionaries'', was later republished by Dark Horse in [[Star Wars Omnibus: Wild Space Volume 2|''Star Wars Omnibus: Wild Space'' Volume 2]]<ref name="Omnibus" /> on [[October 9]], [[2013]].<ref name="Dark Horse">{{DarkHorse|url=Books/18-450/Star-Wars-Omnibus-Wild-Space-Volume-2-TPB|text=Star Wars Omnibus: Wild Space Volume 2 TPB}}</ref> "Celestia Galactica Photografica" was later published by [[Marvel Comics]] in ''[[Star Wars Legends Epic Collection: Legacy Vol. 4]]''<ref name="Legends" /> on March 15, [[2022]].<ref name="Amazon">{{PenguinComics|url=book/?isbn=9781302934507|text=STAR WARS LEGENDS EPIC COLLECTION: LEGACY VOL. 4 TPB|nolive=1}}</ref> The collection placed the artworks between "[[Storyteller (comic story)|Storyteller]]," a comic story<ref name="Legends" /> originally published in [[Star Wars Tales 19|''Star Wars Tales'' 19]] on [[May 14]], [[2004]],<ref name="Storyteller date">{{DarkHorse|url=Comics/12-582/Star-Wars-Tales-19-Photo-cover|text=Star Wars Tales 19}}</ref> and "[[The Fourth Precept]]," another story from ''Visionaries''.<ref name="Legends" />

==Continuity==
{{Quote|{{'}}Celestia Galactica{{'}} by Ryan Church: A series of still-life images touching upon the many worlds and peoples affected by the Clone Wars.|Description of "Celestia Galactica Photografica" from ''Star Wars Insider'' 82, which is at odds with the eras depicted in the gallery's paintings|<ref name="Insider" />}}
[[File:Bridgeworld_Lost.jpg|thumb|left|200px|''Bridgeworld Lost'' is one of several paintings in "Celestia Galactica Photografica" that portrays the Clone Wars.]]
"Celestia Galactica Photografica" portrays images of different events and locations from the ''Star Wars'' universe.<ref name="CGP" /> According to [[Leland Chee]], keeper of the [[Holocron continuity database]], "Celestia Galactica Photografica" exists as a series of paintings [[In-universe|within]] the ''Star Wars'' universe.<ref name="SWBoards">{{SWBoards|url=thread.jspa?threadID=125719&start=0675#10095628|thread=Event Timeline and Character Age Discussion|user=[[Leland Chee|Tasty Taste]]|date=2005-03-29|time=11:02 AM|quote=…the "Celestia Galactica Photografica" exists as works of art within continuity.}}</ref> The magazine [[Star Wars Insider 82|''Star Wars Insider'' 82]], which was shipped to subscribers on [[May 17]], 2005, before its wide release on [[May 30]],<ref name="Insider 82 date">{{SWArchive|url=community/fanclub/mag/news20050506.html|text=The Dark Side Shines in ''Star Wars Insider'' #82}}</ref> provided an overview of ''Visionaries'', which describes "Celestia Galactica Photografica" as portraying the worlds affected by the Clone Wars.<ref name="Insider" /> However, the gallery portrays aspects of the [[Imperial Era/Legends|Imperial Period]], and many of the paintings have no overt connection to the Clone Wars.<ref name="CGP" />

Chronologically, ''Mygeeto Burns'', ''Bridgeworld Lost'', and ''Utapau Surrenders'' portray the events of the Clone Wars,<ref name="CGP" /> which took place in-universe between [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]] and [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]].<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> The former and latter of those paintings tie in to ''Revenge of the Sith'', in which the Pau'an [[Tion Medon/Legends|Tion Medon]] reveals that Utapau has been occupied by Grievous prior to the events of the film, and a scene of the Battle of Mygeeto is shown.<ref name="ROTS">{{Film|III}}</ref> ''Alderaan Refugees'' takes place at an unspecified [[Time/Legends|time]]<ref name="CGP" /> prior to the [[Destruction of Alderaan/Legends|Destruction of Alderaan]] in [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]],<ref name="NEC" /> which was originally portrayed in the [[1977]] original trilogy film, [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']].<ref name="ANH">{{Film|IV}}</ref>

[[File:AT-AT_Villa.jpg|thumb|right|200px|''AT-AT Villa'' portrays a modified vehicle from the original trilogy.]]
''Death Star Drydock'', ''Bad Pet'', and ''Executor Executes'' take place during the Imperial Period. ''Death Star Drydock'' does not specify which version of the Death Star it takes place on, but it, along with ''Bad Pet'', occur during Anakin Skywalker's time inside his [[Darth Vader's armor/Legends|armor]], which began in 19 BBY<ref name="NEC" /> as portrayed in ''Revenge of the Sith''<ref name="ROTS" /> and ended with his [[Death/Legends|death]] in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]]<ref name="NEC" /> in [[1983]]'s [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Star Wars'': Episode VI ''Return of the Jedi'']].<ref name="ROTJ">{{Film|VI}}</ref> ''Executor Executes'' takes place during the lifespan of the ''Executor'',<ref name="CGP" /> first portrayed in [[Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back|''Star Wars'' Episode V ''The Empire Strikes Back'']].<ref name="ESB">{{Film|V}}</ref> In-universe, the ''Executor'' took flight in [[0 ABY/Legends|0.5 ABY]] and was destroyed at the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]] in 4 ABY,<ref name="NEC" /> as portrayed in ''Return of the Jedi''.<ref name="ROTJ" />

''AT-AT Villa'' and ''Reclaimed Wreck'' are set during or after the Imperial Period,<ref name="CGP" /> as they depict vehicles used by the Galactic Empire.<ref name="ESB" /> ''Hunting Overlook'' and ''Green Coruscant'' do not take place in a specified era.<ref name="CGP" /> ''Legacy Vol. 4'' of the [[Epic Collection]] series, which contains "Celestia Galactica Photografica," states that the stories inside take place more than [[Standard century/Legends|one hundred]] [[Standard year/Legends|years]] after the events of ''Return of the Jedi'',<ref name="Legends" /> which takes place in 4 ABY.<ref name="NEC" /> Therefore, the paintings in "Celestia Galactica Photografica" must have been created in-universe after [[104 ABY]], within the [[Legacy era]].<ref name="104 ABY" />

==Media==
